title: Scenario Bank Evaluation Loop
summary: Builds a realistic scenario bank, freezes success criteria, and iterates until the product passes.
steps: List major capabilities, edge cases, user roles, and failure modes before writing test scenarios.
Create realistic scenarios and freeze the success criteria, scoring rubric, and test conditions.
Run every scenario under the same conditions and collect evidence for each outcome.
Fix only root causes shown by failures, then rerun affected scenarios and the complete bank.
Do not weaken the criteria mid-run; create a new evaluation revision if criteria must change.
stopConditions: all scenarios meet the original bar
author: Palette Lab
prompt:
Use the Scenario Bank Evaluation Loop for [capabilities, scenario_count, success_criteria].

First define the task boundary, success criteria, allowed actions, required evidence, risk level, and budget. Work in bounded iterations. In each iteration:
- List major capabilities, edge cases, user roles, and failure modes before writing test scenarios.
- Create realistic scenarios and freeze the success criteria, scoring rubric, and test conditions.
- Run every scenario under the same conditions and collect evidence for each outcome.
- Fix only root causes shown by failures, then rerun affected scenarios and the complete bank.
- Do not weaken the criteria mid-run; create a new evaluation revision if criteria must change.

Quality gates before completion:
- scenario bank covers capabilities
- rubric frozen before testing
- evidence captured for each outcome
- full rerun passes

Stop when one of these is true:
- all scenarios meet the original bar
- criteria revision is required
- budget ends

Finish with:
- scenario table
- rubric
- run logs
- fix log
- final pass summary

Escalate for human approval before: changing success criteria, shipping with known failed scenarios.
